text: If you've been waiting out this week's mugginess, today's your reset button — the front's clearing out, humidity's dropping, and Burlington settles into a genuinely comfortable stretch. Mostly sunny this afternoon near 81, light north wind, low humidity making it feel every bit as pleasant as the number suggests. Models do disagree a bit on today's shower chance (Euro's much wetter than GFS or ICON), but with the front already through and skies clearing, I'd bet on staying dry. Worth noting: moderate air quality today from wildfire smoke drifting down from Canada, so anyone sensitive might want to keep outdoor exertion modest. The lake's in great shape too, water at 74, waves a foot or two, light north wind — a fine evening to be on the water. Saturday's the pick of the weekend: sunny, high near 81, winds light, waves calming further by night. Enjoy this stretch, because the AFD's already flagging a heat spike and possible nocturnal storms arriving early next week.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-10T21:50:00+00:00): Partly Cloudy, 82F feels 82F, humidity 40%, wind NNE 9 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  This Afternoon: Mostly sunny, with a high near 81. North wind around 9 mph.

  Saturday: Sunny, with a high near 81. North wind 3 to 10 mph.
  Saturday Night: Mostly clear, with a low around 59. Northeast wind 1 to 9 mph.

  1. Seasonable and dry weather this weekend before temperatures and humidity increase early next week.
  2. A brief burst of heat will give way to a steadier period of more seasonable weather following a risk of nocturnal severe thunderstorms.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 227 PM EDT Friday... No significant changes.

  Saturday: North winds 5 to 10 knots. Sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.

  2026-07-10: highs [GFS 82, Euro 78, ICON 81] spread 4F; precip chance [GFS 25%, Euro 93%, ICON 55%]
  2026-07-11: highs [GFS 80, Euro 76, ICON 79] spread 4F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 2%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-12: highs [GFS 77, Euro 81, ICON 83] spread 6F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
AIR: AQI 61 Moderate (PM2.5) — On Friday, July 10th, expect moderate air quality across Vermont as smoke from Canadian wildfires continues to impact the region.
SUN: rise 2026-07-10T05:18-04:00, set 2026-07-10T20:38-04:00, UV max 7.55
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high None, low 59: A few clouds. Lows overnight in the upper 50s.
  high 82, low 58: Mix of sun and clouds. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the upper 50s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: BURLINGTON, Vt. (WCAX) - A cold front is finishing its push through the region today, ending our recent shower activity and bringing in a much more comfortable air mass for the weekend. Aside from a stray shower today, mainly over the Adirondacks or Green Mountains, most of the region trends dry with clearing skies and lower humidity. The weekend looks fantastic — sunny, warm, and comfortable. Heat builds again early next week, peaking Tuesday with highs in the mid-90s and a First Alert Weather Day in effect for possible strong storms as a powerful cold front approaches. Behind that front, more seasonable and comfortable summer weather returns by the middle and end of next week.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-09T22:03:00Z): Beautiful weekend, less humid — Turning less humid Friday, beautiful weekend ahead Beautiful weekend, less humid Turning less humid Friday, beautiful weekend ahead
